notes_md:
**bà** = grandmother. It refers to your grandma or an elderly woman.

Vietnamese uses family words like *bà* as pronouns, so it can mean “you” or “she” when talking to/about an older woman. You don’t need a classifier before *bà*.  

To be specific: *bà nội* = dad’s mother, *bà ngoại* = mom’s mother.  

Common pairing: *ông bà* = grandparents (both together).  

It can also mean “Mrs.” or “madam” in polite address to an older woman.

translation.en: I met the grandmother at the market.

exampleSentences: Cuối tuần này, em sẽ về quê thăm bà cùng cả gia đình.
Bà thường kể cho chị nghe nhiều câu chuyện cổ tích rất hay.
Mỗi sáng, bà ra vườn tưới hoa và cho gà ăn.
notes:
bà = grandmother. It refers to your grandma or an elderly woman.

Vietnamese uses family words like bà as pronouns, so it can mean “you” or “she” when talking to/about an older woman. You don’t need a classifier before bà.  

To be specific: bà nội = dad’s mother, bà ngoại = mom’s mother.  

Common pairing: ông bà = grandparents (both together).  

It can also mean “Mrs.” or “madam” in polite address to an older woman.

example.es: Mi abuela ama mucho a la familia.
example.vi: Bà tôi rất yêu thương gia đình.
example.en: My grandmother loves the family very much.

definitions.es: Un término utilizado para referirse a una abuela o a una mujer anciana.
definitions.vi: Một thuật ngữ dùng để chỉ bà hoặc một người phụ nữ lớn tuổi.
definitions.en: A term used to refer to a grandmother or an elderly woman.

translation.en: I met the grandmother at the market.

exampleSentencesTranslated: This weekend, I will visit my grandmother with the whole family.
Grandmother often tells me many interesting fairy tales.
Every morning, grandmother goes to the garden to water flowers and feed the chickens.
